title: Narayana Kavacham
intro: Read Narayana Kavacham with Hindi meaning. Chant this sacred hymn to receive Lord Vishnu’s protection and blessings.
description:
Narayana Kavacham

॥ Raja Uvacha ॥
1. Yaya guptah sahasrakshah savahan ripusainikan.
Kridanniva vinirjitya trilokya bubhuje shriyam..

2. Bhagavamstanmamakhyahi varma narayanatmakam.
Yathas-tatayinah shatrun yena guptojayanmridhe..

॥ Sri Suka Uvacha ॥
3. Vritah purohitostvashtro mahendrayanupruchhate.
Narayanakhyam varmaha tadihaikamanah shrinu..

॥ Visvarupa Uvacha ॥
4. Dhautanghripanirachamya sapavitra udang mukhah.
Kritasvangakaranyaso mantrabhyam vagyatah shuchih..

5. Narayanamayam varma san-nahyed bhaya agate.
Padayorjanunoru-vorudare hridyathorasih..

6. Mukhe shirasyanupurvyad-omkaradini vinyaset.
Om namo narayanayeti viparyayamathapi va..

7. Karanyasam tatah kuryad dvadashaksharavidyaya.
Pranavadiyakarantam-angulyangushthaparvasu..

8. Nyased hridaya omkaram vikaramanu murdhani.
Shakaram tu bhruvormadhye nakaram shikhaya dishet..

9. Vekaram netrayoryunjyan-nakaram sarvasandhishu.
Makaramastramud-dishya mantramurtirbhaved budhah..

10. Savisargam phadantam tat sarvadikshu vinirdishet.
Om vishnave nama iti..

11. Atmanam paramam dhyayed dhyeyam shat-shaktibhiryutam.
Vidyatejastapomurtimimam mantramudaharet..

12. Om Harirvidadhyanmama sarvaraksham nyastanghripadmah patagendraprishthe.
Dararicharmasigadeshuchapashan dadhanoshtagunoshtabahuh..

13. Jaleshu mam rakshatu matsyamurtiryadoganebhyo varunasya pashat.
Sthaleshu mayavatuvamano-vyat trivikramah khe-vatu vishvarupah..

14. Durgeshvatavyajimukhadishu prabhah payannrisimhosura-yuthaparih.
Vimunchato yasya mahattahasam disho vinedurnyapatanshcha garbhah..

15. Rakshatvasau madhvani yajnakalpah svadamshtrayonnitadharo varahah.
Ramos-drikuteshatha vipravase salakshmanov-yad bharatagrajosman..

16. Mamugradharmadakhilat pramadannarayanah patu narashcha hasat.
Dattastvayogadatha yoganathah payad guneshah kapilah karmabandhat..

17. Sanatkumaro vatu kamadevaddhayashirsha mam pathi devahelanat.
Devarshivaryah purusharchanantarat kurmo harirma mam nirayadasheshat..

18. Dhanvantarirbhagavan patvapathyad dvandvad bhayad-rishabho nirjitatahma.
Yajnashcha lokadavat-ajjanantad balo ganat krodhavashadahindrah..

19. Dvaipayano bhagavanaprabohad buddhastu pakhandaganat pramadat.
Kalkih kale kalamalat prapatu dharmavanayorukritavatarah..

20. Mam keshavo gadaya prataravyad govinda asangavamattavenuh.
Narayana prahna udattashaktirmadhyandine vishnurarindrapanih..

21. Devoparahne madhuhogradhanva sayam tridhamavatu madhavo mam.
Doshe hrishikesha utardharatre nishitha eko-vatu padmanabhah..

22. Srivatsadhamapararatra ishah pratyusha ishosidharo janardanah.
Damodarov-yadanusandhyam prabhate vishveshvaro bhagavan kalamurtih..

23. Chakram yugantanalatigmanemi bhramat samantad bhagavatprayuktam.
Dandagdhi dandagdhyarisainyamasu kaksham yatha vatasakho hutashah..

24. Gadeshani-sparshana-visphulinge nishpindhi nishpindhyajitapriyasi.
Kushmandavainayakayaksharakshobhutagrahamshchurnaya churnayarin..

25. Tvam yatudhanapramathapretamatripishachavipragrahaghoradrishtin.
Darendra vidravaya krishnapurito bhimasvano-rerhridayani kampayan..

26. Tvam tigmadharasivararisainyamishaprayukto mama chindhi chindhi.
Charmanchhata-chandra chhadaya dvishamaghonam hara papachakshusham..

27. Yanno bhayam grahebhyo bhut ketubhyo nribhya eva cha.
Sarisripebhyo damshtribhyo bhutebhyon-hobhya eva va..

28. Sarvanyetani bhagannamarupastrakirtanat.
Prayantu samkshayam sadyo ye nah shreyah pratipakah..

29. Garudo bhagavan stotrastobhashchandomayah prabhuh.
Rakshatvasheshakrichchrebhyo vishvaksenah svanamabhih..

30. Sarvapadbhyo harernamarupayanayudhani nah.
Buddhindriyamanah pranan pantu parshadabhushanah..

31. Yatha hi bhagavaneva vastutah sadsachcha yat.
Satyananena nah sarve yantu nashamupadravah..

32. Yathaikatmyanubhavanam vikalparahitah svayam.
Bhushanayudhalingakhyah dhatte shaktih svamayaya..

33. Tenaiva satyamanena sarvajno bhagavan harih.
Patu sarvaih svarupairnah sada sarvatra sarvagah..

34. Vidikshu dikshurdhvamadhah samantadantarbahirbhagavan narasimhah.
Prahapayan-lokabhayam svanena grastasamatastatejah..

35. Maghavannidamakhayatam varma narayanatmakam.
Vijeshyasyanjasa yena danshito-surayuthapan..

36. Etad dharayamanastu yam yam pashyati chakshusha.
Pada va sansprishet sadyah sadhvasat sa vimuchyate..

37. Na kutashchit bhayam tasya vidyam dharayato bhavet.
Rajadasyugrahadibhyo vyaghradibhyashcha karhichit..

38. Imam vidyam pura kashchit kaushiko dharayan dvijah.
Yogadharanaya svangam jahau sa marudhanvani..

39. Tasyopari vimanena gandharvapatirekada.
Yayau chitrarathah stribhirvrito yatra dvijakshayah..

40. Gaganan-nyapatat sadyah savimano hyavak shirah.
Sa valakhilyavachanadasthinyadaya vismitah.
Prasya prachisarasvatyam snatva dhama svamanvaghat..

41. Ya idam shrinuyat kale yo dharayati chadritah.
Tam namasyanti bhutani muchyate sarvato bhayat..

42. Etam vidyamadhigato vishvarupachchhatakratuh.
Trailokyalakshmim bubhuje vinirjityamridhesuran..

॥ Iti Sri Narayana Kavacham Sampurnam ॥

faq.a1: Narayana Kavacham is a sacred and powerful protective prayer dedicated to Lord Narayana (Vishnu). It is found in the 6th Canto of the Srimad Bhagavatam and is considered an "armor" of spiritual protection.
faq.q2: Who originally taught the Narayana Kavacham?
faq.a2: According to the Srimad Bhagavatam, it was taught by Vishwarupa (the priest of the Devas) to Lord Indra to help him overcome the Asuras and regain his kingdom.
faq.q3: What are the benefits of reciting Narayana Kavacham?
faq.a3: Devotees believe that reciting this Kavacham provides protection against fear, enemies, negative energies, accidents, and psychological distress. It is said to grant inner strength and peace of mind.
faq.q4: Is there a specific time to recite this prayer?
faq.a4: While it can be recited at any time, it is most beneficial when one feels fearful, stressed, or is facing difficult situations. Many practitioners recite it in the early morning for daily protection.
faq.q5: Are there any strict rules for reciting it?
faq.a5: The most important requirement is a pure heart and devotion. While the text mentions specific rituals like 'Nyasa', the essence lies in sincere faith and surrender to Lord Narayana.
